trivia_bullets.text: Redcaps derive from Anglo-Scottish border folklore: malevolent goblins inhabiting ruined castles who soak their caps in victims' blood and must kill regularly to survive. The tradition dates to medieval border-war territory, roughly the 13th-16th centuries.

In ELD lore, redcaps are Wilds creatures — chaotic, 'barrel-height' humanoids outside the five courts' chivalric system. The creature type Goblin Knight complicates this playfully, like Ogre Errant's Ogre Knight type.
"Many tales of redcap terror begin with two simple things: bloodlust and stolen steel." — No named speaker. 'Stolen steel' references folklore: redcap weapons are always pilfered from victims.
Double strike on a 1/2 body for 2R. Efficient for limited in ELD, where first-strike and regular combat damage together can trade up profitably. Color identity is mono-red despite the dark cave art.

related_cards.name: Fling
related_cards.note: Giants throw redcaps at knights; this card is the creature being thrown

related_cards.name: Wydwen, the Biting Gale
related_cards.note: Earlier Lorwyn-block redcap design precedent in MTG creature-type history

related_cards.name: Bloodsoaked Champion
related_cards.note: Thematic parallel: creature bound to keep fighting, mirroring the folklore 'must kill to survive' rule
